retrieved.text: Image 1 of 1 The main building of the University of Vienna houses several institutes, such as the Institute of History, as well as central service facilities, such as the University Library. © University of Vienna / Alex Schuppich The arcade courtyard can be found inside the main building: Here you can learn in good weather or just enjoy the sun. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair The largest lecture hall in the main building of the University of Vienna is the Audimax, which offers more than 750 seats in the hall and on the balcony. © University of Vienna / Franz Pfluegl You can also learn and work well in other locations of the University Library - the Departmental Libraries - for example in the Education, Linguistics and Comparative Literature Library in Sensengasse 3a. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair A location of the University of Vienna near the main building is the NIG - the New Institute Building. retrieved.title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
retrieved.text:
"Team Barrierefrei" offers counselling and information related to studying and student life for students with a disability, impairment or disease.

- [Studying with disabilities](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [University of Vienna Accessbile Studies](/en/)

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

The cafeterias offer reasonable meals. Everyone is allowed to eat there, a permit or special ID is not needed. To get meals even cheaper you can obtain the so-called "Mensenpickerl" at the Austrian National Union of Students (Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft - ÖH).

Apart from the cafeterias, students like to go to the numerous restaurants and cafés located around the main building where meals and snacks are relatively cheap.

- [Cafeterias](http://www.mensen.at/) - good food for a reasonable price
- [ÖH - Mensenpickerl](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/page/canteen-sticker)- eat even cheaper

As long as you are studying at the University of Vienna you have to pay the fees according to your citizenship (in some cases only the Students' Union fee, in some cases more) every semester in time. The tuition fee depends on your citzenship and, for EU/EEA citizens, also on the duration of your studies.

- [Students' Union fee/tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

There are several types of financial support available for students. Obtain information about allowances/grants for studying abroad in your home country. Student discounts can make life easier - at least in financial terms.

retrieved.title: Health during your studies
retrieved.text:
Just five minutes of exercise in green spaces reduce stress and negative moods.

Strengthen your everyday student life with small, conscious decisions about nutrition.

Eating a small handful of nuts every day can have a positive effect on heart health.

Vegetarian and vegan dishes are available daily at two locations at the University of Vienna.

- [Lia Cafeteria on Oskar-Morgenstern-Platz](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-oskar-morgenstern-platz-1/)
- [Lia Bistro in the University Biology Building](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-djerassiplatz-1-ubb/)

[From master´s thesis to optimised nutrition](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studierende/ernaehrung-im-studium/)

In this post, student Denny explains how to eat healthier in everyday student life.

Well-being starts in the mind. Strategies to strengthen resilience and focus on studying can help.

[Tips and tricks for dealing with stressful periods of study can be found on the blog.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/facing-challenges-wie-kann-ich-meine-resilienz-staerken/)

[Decisiveness, control over your own reactions and more.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/resilienz-im-studium-strategien-fuer-mehr-handlungskompetenz/)

heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
retrieved.text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

retrieved.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
retrieved.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
retrieved.text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

- [#univie communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/#c487599)
- [Summer & Winter Schools](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/univie-summer/winter-schools)
- [Student Mobility](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Diversity portal of the University of Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Accessible studying at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/) (in German)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

The University of Vienna is also the largest research and educational institution in Austria with a total of 7,400 academics. Our teaching staff adheres to the principle of research-led teaching in their courses. They impart knowledge about latest research findings and their students are able to participate in original research. The University of Vienna sets future-oriented accents in interdisciplinary innovative research fields.

- [Financial support by the University of Vienna](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/) (in German)
- [Research Services and Career Development](https://forschung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [The University of Vienna´s strategic priorities](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/research)

retrieved.title: Incoming students with disabilities
heading_path: FAQ for incoming students with disabilities
retrieved.text: schwerhörige oder gehörlose Studierende? [GESTU](https://www.uniability.org/studium/studienbezogene-serviceeinrichtungen/gestu-gehoerlos-studieren/)bietet Unterstützung durch Gebärdensprachdolmetscher\*innen, Schriftdolmetscher\*innen oder Unterstützung Studierender für Mitschriften. - Is personal assistance available? The University of Vienna does not offer personal assistance. For cases where state-funded personal assistance is not available or insufficient, the Accessible Studying team coordinates a project called “ISU”. As part of this project, students enrolled in a teacher education programme provide support to students with disabilities or chronic illnesses. You have to apply in advance. Please check the deadline or [contact the Accessible Studying team](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). - Is there accessible transportation around the University? The University of Vienna is not located on a university campus. Courses take place at many different university locations throughout Vienna. You might have to access different buildings and use public transport to reach them. Vienna has a well-developed public transport network. Almost all busses, trains and stations are wheelchair-accessible, but some of the trams are not. You will find tactile guidance orientation in and around many of the stations and stops. Most sidewalks are barrier-free. If you prefer cycling, Vienna is a bike-friendly city. Please note that tickets for public transport are not included in tuition fees. [Information about public transport](https://www.wienerlinien.at/web/wl-en) - What is the Austrian National Union of Students? You can also contact the accessibility department of the Austrian National Union of Students (“Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft – ÖH”), which is elected by students studying in Austria.

heading_path: Further offers for pupils and school classes at the University of Vienna
Grüne Schule at the Botanical Garden
retrieved.text:
Who knows the trick of the lotus blossom? What does a living fossil look like? Where does Vienna's biggest bamboo grove grow?

The Botanical Garden of the University of Vienna offers school classes a unique opportunity to experience nature and explore plants in the middle of the city.

The aim of the project Grüne Schule (Green School) is to give pupils an understanding of the fascinating world of plants. Guided tours and workshops stimulate the interest in researching and exploring nature. Expertise, stunning details and inspiring histories as well as the didactic mediation take centre stage - an exciting and adecuate provision of knowledge for our young generation.

[Click here to know more](http://grueneschule.univie.ac.at) (in German)

retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna is one of the most prestiguous and largest universities in Europe and offers a wide range of degree programmes. From education, social sciences, philosophy and religion, history, society, politics and arts over culture, media, mathematics, informatics and technology, natural and life sciences, psychology, health and sports tp languages, economic sciences and law.

This diversity gives students the opportunity to choose an individualised way through their studies that best suits their interests and abilities.

The cafeterias offer reasonable meals. Everyone is allowed to eat there, a permit or special ID is not needed. To get meals even cheaper you can obtain the so-called "Mensenpickerl" at the Austrian National Union of Students (Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft - ÖH).

Apart from the cafeterias, students like to go to the numerous restaurants and cafés located around the main building where meals and snacks are relatively cheap.

- [Cafeterias](http://www.mensen.at/) - good food for a reasonable price
- [ÖH - Mensenpickerl](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/page/canteen-sticker)- eat even cheaper

As long as you are studying at the University of Vienna you have to pay the fees according to your citizenship (in some cases only the Students' Union fee, in some cases more) every semester in time. The tuition fee depends on your citzenship and, for EU/EEA citizens, also on the duration of your studies.

- [Students' Union fee/tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

There are several types of financial support available for students. Obtain information about allowances/grants for studying abroad in your home country. Student discounts can make life easier - at least in financial terms.

reranked.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
reranked.text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

- [#univie communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/#c487599)
- [Summer & Winter Schools](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/univie-summer/winter-schools)
- [Student Mobility](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Diversity portal of the University of Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Accessible studying at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/) (in German)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

The University of Vienna is also the largest research and educational institution in Austria with a total of 7,400 academics. Our teaching staff adheres to the principle of research-led teaching in their courses. They impart knowledge about latest research findings and their students are able to participate in original research. The University of Vienna sets future-oriented accents in interdisciplinary innovative research fields.

reranked.title: Incoming students with disabilities
heading_path: FAQ for incoming students with disabilities
reranked.text: schwerhörige oder gehörlose Studierende? [GESTU](https://www.uniability.org/studium/studienbezogene-serviceeinrichtungen/gestu-gehoerlos-studieren/)bietet Unterstützung durch Gebärdensprachdolmetscher\*innen, Schriftdolmetscher\*innen oder Unterstützung Studierender für Mitschriften. - Is personal assistance available? The University of Vienna does not offer personal assistance. For cases where state-funded personal assistance is not available or insufficient, the Accessible Studying team coordinates a project called “ISU”. As part of this project, students enrolled in a teacher education programme provide support to students with disabilities or chronic illnesses. You have to apply in advance. Please check the deadline or [contact the Accessible Studying team](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). - Is there accessible transportation around the University? The University of Vienna is not located on a university campus. Courses take place at many different university locations throughout Vienna. You might have to access different buildings and use public transport to reach them. Vienna has a well-developed public transport network. Almost all busses, trains and stations are wheelchair-accessible, but some of the trams are not. You will find tactile guidance orientation in and around many of the stations and stops. Most sidewalks are barrier-free. If you prefer cycling, Vienna is a bike-friendly city. Please note that tickets for public transport are not included in tuition fees. [Information about public transport](https://www.wienerlinien.at/web/wl-en) - What is the Austrian National Union of Students? You can also contact the accessibility department of the Austrian National Union of Students (“Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft – ÖH”), which is elected by students studying in Austria.

Grüne Schule at the Botanical Garden
reranked.text:
Who knows the trick of the lotus blossom? What does a living fossil look like? Where does Vienna's biggest bamboo grove grow?

The Botanical Garden of the University of Vienna offers school classes a unique opportunity to experience nature and explore plants in the middle of the city.

The aim of the project Grüne Schule (Green School) is to give pupils an understanding of the fascinating world of plants. Guided tours and workshops stimulate the interest in researching and exploring nature. Expertise, stunning details and inspiring histories as well as the didactic mediation take centre stage - an exciting and adecuate provision of knowledge for our young generation.

- [All locations of the University of Vienna](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/locations-maps/university-of-vienna-locations/)
- [Student Space: room for students](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/)
- [Blog of the University of Vienna](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/)

selected.title: Health during your studies
selected.text:
Just five minutes of exercise in green spaces reduce stress and negative moods.

Strengthen your everyday student life with small, conscious decisions about nutrition.

Eating a small handful of nuts every day can have a positive effect on heart health.

Vegetarian and vegan dishes are available daily at two locations at the University of Vienna.

- [Lia Cafeteria on Oskar-Morgenstern-Platz](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-oskar-morgenstern-platz-1/)
- [Lia Bistro in the University Biology Building](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-djerassiplatz-1-ubb/)

[From master´s thesis to optimised nutrition](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studierende/ernaehrung-im-studium/)

In this post, student Denny explains how to eat healthier in everyday student life.

[1] Why study at the University of Vienna? — Uni Insights
Image 1 of 1 The main building of the University of Vienna houses several institutes, such as the Institute of History, as well as central service facilities, such as the University Library. © University of Vienna / Alex Schuppich The arcade courtyard can be found inside the main building: Here you can learn in good weather or just enjoy the sun. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair The largest lecture hall in the main building of the University of Vienna is the Audimax, which offers more than 750 seats in the hall and on the balcony. © University of Vienna / Franz Pfluegl You can also learn and work well in other locations of the University Library - the Departmental Libraries - for example in the Education, Linguistics and Comparative Literature Library in Sensengasse 3a. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair A location of the University of Vienna near the main building is the NIG - the New Institute Building. Many University Departments are located here like the Department of Cultural and Social Anthropology or of Philosophy. © University of Vienna / Peter Wienerroither Another location of the University of Vienna is the Campus. Here you will find, for example, the Department of Art History or of Romance Studies. With its many green courtyards, the Campus offers space for rest and relaxation in the middle of the city.

[2] Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
"Team Barrierefrei" offers counselling and information related to studying and student life for students with a disability, impairment or disease.

- [Studying with disabilities](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [University of Vienna Accessbile Studies](/en/)

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

The cafeterias offer reasonable meals. Everyone is allowed to eat there, a permit or special ID is not needed. To get meals even cheaper you can obtain the so-called "Mensenpickerl" at the Austrian National Union of Students (Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft - ÖH).

Apart from the cafeterias, students like to go to the numerous restaurants and cafés located around the main building where meals and snacks are relatively cheap.

- [Cafeterias](http://www.mensen.at/) - good food for a reasonable price
- [ÖH - Mensenpickerl](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/page/canteen-sticker)- eat even cheaper

As long as you are studying at the University of Vienna you have to pay the fees according to your citizenship (in some cases only the Students' Union fee, in some cases more) every semester in time. The tuition fee depends on your citzenship and, for EU/EEA citizens, also on the duration of your studies.

- [Students' Union fee/tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

There are several types of financial support available for students. Obtain information about allowances/grants for studying abroad in your home country. Student discounts can make life easier - at least in financial terms.

- [Student Space: room for students](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/)
- [Blog of the University of Vienna](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair
